info.title: oore.build API
info.description:
REST API for oore.build — a self-hosted, Flutter-first mobile CI and internal app distribution platform.

The backend daemon (`oored`) exposes this API on the configured listen address. All endpoints under `/v1/` use JSON request/response bodies unless noted otherwise.

## Authentication

- **Setup endpoints** (`/v1/setup/*`) are token-gated by a bootstrap session token and auto-disabled after setup completes.
- **Auth endpoints** (`/v1/auth/*`) support local-mode login and OIDC login/logout flows.
- **All other endpoints** require a valid session token via `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.
- **Runner endpoints** use a separate runner token for authentication.

## Base URL

Since oore.build is self-hosted, the base URL is your daemon's listen address (e.g. `http://localhost:8787`).

get.description: Returns `{"ok": true}` when the daemon is running.

post.description: Returns a signed download URL for an artifact. URLs expire after 15 minutes.

Creates a local-mode session without OIDC.
If setup is still pending in local mode, first login auto-finalizes
local owner bootstrap.

403.description: Blocked by mode policy or non-loopback source

post.description: Invalidates the current session.
Exchanges the authorization code for tokens and creates a session.

requestBody.description: Authorization code and state from OIDC provider

get.description:
Initiates the OIDC authorization code flow for user login.
Only available after setup is complete.
Returns builds, optionally filtered by project, pipeline, or status.

post.description: Cancels a queued or running build.

get.description:
Server-sent event stream of build logs. Requires a short-lived streaming
token obtained from `POST /v1/builds/{build_id}/stream-token`.

post.description:
Exchanges a session token for a short-lived (5 min) streaming token
suitable for use in SSE query parameters.

get.description: Returns all SCM integrations, optionally filtered by provider.

post.description: Finalises the GitHub App manifest flow after the redirect back from GitHub.
Returns a URL to navigate the browser to for GitHub App manifest creation.
Creates a GitLab integration with OAuth or personal token auth.

get.summary: Browse local directories for local repository registration

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Absolute directory path to browse. Defaults to the daemon user's home directory

post.description: Fetches new installations from GitHub and syncs their repositories.
Validates a pipeline configuration without persisting it.

put.description: Upload keystores and configure debug/release signing profiles.

Upload certificates, provisioning profiles, and API credentials.
Request body limit: 10 MiB.

post.description: Registers a device UDID and optionally triggers a provisioning profile sync.
Re-downloads provisioning profiles from Apple via App Store Connect API.
Queues a new build for the specified project.

409.description: Project source is not configured or resolvable

get.description:
Returns the current setup state and instance ID. This endpoint is
always public and contains no sensitive information.

post.description: Registers a new build runner and returns a runner authentication token.
Runner claims the next queued build. Returns `null` job if no builds are available.
Sends a heartbeat from a runner to report its status. Authenticated via runner token.

get.description: Runner checks the current status of its assigned build.

post.description: Runner creates an artifact record and gets a signed upload URL.
Runner appends log chunks to a build. Max 10,000 lines per build, 4KB per line.
Runner reports build status transitions and step results.

put.description: Configure the storage backend for build artifacts (local, S3, or R2).

get.description: Returns check-by-check readiness required before enabling External Access (`runtime_mode=remote`).

put.description: Update instance-wide preferences (e.g. key storage mode).

400.description: External Access preflight failed or unsupported values

403.description: Owner-only mode change attempted by non-owner

post.description:
Exchanges a one-time bootstrap token (generated by the CLI) for a
setup session token. Rate-limited: 5 failed attempts per token hash
triggers lockout.

Finalises the setup flow, transitions state to `ready`, inserts the
owner into the users table, and clears the setup session.
Creates the setup owner without OIDC when runtime mode is `local`.

Performs OIDC discovery on the provided issuer URL and stores the
provider configuration. Requires a valid setup session.

Initiates the OIDC authorization code flow to create the instance owner.
Returns an authorization URL for the frontend to redirect to.

Completes the setup OIDC flow by exchanging the authorization code
for tokens and creating the owner account.

get.description:
Returns a summary of current setup configuration. Requires a valid
setup session.
Returns all users. Requires `owner` or `admin` role.

post.description: Creates a new user with `invited` status. Requires `owner` or `admin` role.

get.description: Returns the profile of the currently authenticated user.

delete.description: Soft-deletes a user. Requires `owner` or `admin` role.

post.description: Re-enables a previously disabled user.

patch.description: Changes a user's role. Requires `owner` or `admin` role.

post.description:
Receives push/PR events from GitHub and triggers matching pipelines.
Authenticated via webhook signature verification.

Receives push/MR events from GitLab and triggers matching pipelines.
Authenticated via webhook secret token.

redirect_url.description: Frontend URL to redirect to after GitHub App creation completes.

create_url.description: URL to navigate the browser to — serves an auto-submitting form that POSTs the manifest to GitHub.

tags.name: Setup
tags.description: Initial instance setup flow (bootstrap token → mode-aware owner creation). Auto-disabled after setup completes.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Mode-aware authentication and session management. Enabled only after setup is complete.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: User management — invite, list, update roles, disable/re-enable.

tags.name: Instance Settings
tags.description: Instance-wide configuration — artifact storage, key storage preferences.

tags.name: Integrations
tags.description: SCM integrations — local git, GitHub App, and GitLab.

tags.name: Projects
tags.description: Project CRUD — each project groups one or more pipelines.

tags.name: Pipelines
tags.description: Pipeline configuration — build platforms, commands, triggers, concurrency.

tags.name: Pipeline Signing
tags.description: Code signing configuration — Android keystores, iOS certificates/profiles.

tags.name: Builds
tags.description: Build lifecycle — queue, list, detail, cancel.

tags.name: Runners
tags.description: Build runner management — register, heartbeat, job claim/status.

tags.name: Build Logs
tags.description: Build log ingestion and retrieval — append, paginated fetch, SSE streaming.

tags.name: Artifacts
tags.description: Build artifact management — upload, list, download via signed URLs.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Incoming webhook receivers for GitHub and GitLab.
